NKJV.1: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes came together to Him, having come from Jerusalem.
NKJV.2: Now when they saw some of His disciples eat bread with defiled, that is, with unwashed hands, they found fault.
NKJV.3: For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands in a special way, holding the tradition of the elders.
NKJV.4: When they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other things which they have received and hold, like the washing of cups, pitchers, copper vessels, and couches.
NKJV.5: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked Him, “Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashed hands?”
NKJV.6: He answered and said to them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written: ‘This people honors Me with their lips, But their heart is far from Me.
NKJV.7: And in vain they worship Me, Teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’
NKJV.8: For laying aside the commandment of God, you hold the tradition of men —the washing of pitchers and cups, and many other such things you do.”
NKJV.9: He said to them, “All too well you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.
NKJV.10: For Moses said,  ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and,  ‘He who curses father or mother, let him be put to death.’
NKJV.11: But you say, ‘If a man says to his father or mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban”—’ (that is, a gift to God),
NKJV.12: then you no longer let him do anything for his father or his mother,
NKJV.13: making the word of God of no effect through your tradition which you have handed down. And many such things you do.”
NKJV.14: When He had called all the multitude to Himself, He said to them, “Hear Me, everyone, and understand:
NKJV.15: There is nothing that enters a man from outside which can defile him; but the things which come out of him, those are the things that  defile a man.
NKJV.16: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!”
NKJV.17: When He had entered a house away from the crowd, His disciples asked Him concerning the parable.
NKJV.18: So He said to them, “Are you thus without understanding also? Do you not perceive that whatever enters a man from outside cannot defile him,
NKJV.19: because it does not enter his heart but his stomach, and is eliminated, thus purifying all foods?”
NKJV.20: And He said, “What comes out of a man, that defiles a man.
NKJV.21: For from within, out of the heart of men,  proceed evil thoughts,  adulteries,  fornications, murders,
NKJV.22: thefts,  covetousness, wickedness,  deceit,  lewdness, an evil eye,  blasphemy,  pride, foolishness.
NKJV.23: All these evil things come from within and defile a man.”
NKJV.24: From there He arose and went to the region of Tyre and Sidon. And He entered a house and wanted no one to know it, but He could not be hidden.
NKJV.25: For a woman whose young daughter had an unclean spirit heard about Him, and she came and fell at His feet.
NKJV.26: The woman was a Greek, a Syro-Phoenician by birth, and she kept asking Him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
NKJV.27: But Jesus said to her, “Let the children be filled first, for it is not good to take the children’s bread and throw it to the little dogs.”
NKJV.28: And she answered and said to Him, “Yes, Lord, yet even the little dogs under the table eat from the children’s crumbs.”
NKJV.29: Then He said to her, “For this saying go your way; the demon has gone out of your daughter.”
NKJV.30: And when she had come to her house, she found the demon gone out, and her daughter lying on the bed.
NKJV.31: Again, departing from the region of Tyre and Sidon, He came through the midst of the region of Decapolis to the Sea of Galilee.
NKJV.32: Then they brought to Him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech, and they begged Him to put His hand on him.
NKJV.33: And He took him aside from the multitude, and put His fingers in his ears, and He spat and touched his tongue.
NKJV.34: Then, looking up to heaven, He sighed, and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
NKJV.35: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was loosed, and he spoke plainly.
NKJV.36: Then He commanded them that they should tell no one; but the more He commanded them, the more widely they proclaimed it.
NKJV.37: And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He makes both the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”

ESV.1: Now when the Pharisees gathered to him, with some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem,
ESV.2: they saw that some of his disciples ate with hands that were defiled, that is, unwashed.
ESV.3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands properly,  holding to the tradition of the elders,
ESV.4: and when they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash.  And there are many other traditions that they observe, such as the washing of cups and pots and copper vessels and dining couches. )
ESV.5: And the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
ESV.6: And he said to them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written,  “‘This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me;
ESV.7: in vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’
ESV.8: You leave the commandment of God and hold to the tradition of men.”
ESV.9: And he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to establish your tradition!
ESV.10: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever reviles father or mother must surely die.’
ESV.11: But you say, ‘If a man tells his father or his mother, “Whatever you would have gained from me is Corban”’ (that is, given to God)  —
ESV.12: then you no longer permit him to do anything for his father or mother,
ESV.13: thus making void the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And many such things you do.”
ESV.14: And he called the people to him again and said to them,  “Hear me, all of you, and understand:
ESV.15: There is nothing outside a person that by going into him can defile him, but the things that come out of a person are what defile him.”
ESV.17: And when he had entered the house and left the people, his disciples asked him about the parable.
ESV.18: And he said to them, “Then are you also without understanding? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him,
ESV.19: since it enters not his heart but his stomach, and is expelled?”  (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
ESV.20: And he said,  “What comes out of a person is what defiles him.
ESV.21: For from within, out of the heart of man, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,
ESV.22: coveting, wickedness, deceit, sensuality, envy, slander, pride, foolishness.
ESV.23: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
ESV.24: And from there he arose and went away to the region of Tyre and Sidon.  And he entered a house and did not want anyone to know, yet he could not be hidden.
ESV.25: But immediately a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit heard of him and came and fell down at his feet.
ESV.26: Now the woman was a Gentile, a Syrophoenician by birth. And she begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
ESV.27: And he said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.”
ESV.28: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord; yet even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs.”
ESV.29: And he said to her, “For this statement you may go your way; the demon has left your daughter.”
ESV.30: And she went home and found the child lying in bed and the demon gone.
ESV.31: Then he returned from the region of Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
ESV.32: And they brought to him a man who was deaf and had a speech impediment, and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
ESV.33: And taking him aside from the crowd privately, he put his fingers into his ears, and after spitting touched his tongue.
ESV.34: And looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
ESV.35: And his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
ESV.36: And Jesus  charged them to tell no one. But the more he charged them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
ESV.37: And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak.”

NASB.1: The Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered around Him when they had come from Jerusalem,
NASB.2: and had seen that some of His disciples were eating their bread with impure hands, that is, unwashed.
NASB.3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they carefully wash their hands, [thus] observing the traditions of the elders;
NASB.4: and [when they come] from the market place, they do not eat unless they cleanse themselves; and there are many other things which they have received in order to observe, such as the washing of cups and pitchers and copper pots.)
NASB.5: The Pharisees and the scribes asked Him, 'Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with impure hands?'
NASB.6: And He said to them, 'Rightly did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written: 'THIS PEOPLE HONORS ME WITH THEIR LIPS, BUT THEIR HEART IS FAR AWAY FROM ME.
NASB.7: 'BUT IN VAIN DO THEY WORSHIP ME, TEACHING AS DOCTRINES THE PRECEPTS OF MEN.'
NASB.8: 'Neglecting the commandment of God, you hold to the tradition of men.'
NASB.9: He was also saying to them, 'You are experts at setting aside the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition.
NASB.10: 'For Moses said, 'HONOR YOUR FATHER AND YOUR MOTHER'; and, 'HE WHO SPEAKS EVIL OF FATHER OR MOTHER, IS TO BE PUT TO DEATH';
NASB.11: but you say, 'If a man says to [his] father or [his] mother, whatever I have that would help you is Corban (that is to say, given [to God]),'
NASB.12: you no longer permit him to do anything for [his] father or [his] mother;
NASB.13: [thus] invalidating the word of God by your tradition which you have handed down; and you do many things such as that.'
NASB.14: After He called the crowd to Him again, He [began] saying to them, 'Listen to Me, all of you, and understand:
NASB.15: there is nothing outside the man which can defile him if it goes into him; but the things which proceed out of the man are what defile the man.
NASB.16: ['If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.]'
NASB.17: When he had left the crowd [and] entered the house, His disciples questioned Him about the parable.
NASB.18: And He said to them, 'Are you so lacking in understanding also? Do you not understand that whatever goes into the man from outside cannot defile him,
NASB.19: because it does not go into his heart, but into his stomach, and is eliminated?' ([Thus He] declared all foods clean.)
NASB.20: And He was saying, 'That which proceeds out of the man, that is what defiles the man.
NASB.21: 'For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed the evil thoughts, fornications, thefts, murders, adulteries,
NASB.22: deeds of coveting [and] wickedness, [as well] [as] deceit, sensuality, envy, slander, pride [and] foolishness.
NASB.23: 'All these evil things proceed from within and defile the man.'
NASB.24: Jesus got up and went away from there to the region of Tyre. And when He had entered a house, He wanted no one to know [of it]; yet He could not escape notice.
NASB.25: But after hearing of Him, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately came and fell at His feet.
NASB.26: Now the woman was a Gentile, of the Syrophoenician race. And she kept asking Him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
NASB.27: And He was saying to her, 'Let the children be satisfied first, for it is not good to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.'
NASB.28: But she answered and said to Him, 'Yes, Lord, [but] even the dogs under the table feed on the children's crumbs.'
NASB.29: And He said to her, 'Because of this answer go; the demon has gone out of your daughter.'
NASB.30: And going back to her home, she found the child lying on the bed, the demon having left.
NASB.31: Again He went out from the region of Tyre, and came through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, within the region of Decapolis.
NASB.32: They brought to Him one who was deaf and spoke with difficulty, and they implored Him to lay His hand on him.
NASB.33: Jesus took him aside from the crowd, by himself, and put His fingers into his ears, and after spitting, He touched his tongue [with the saliva];
NASB.34: and looking up to heaven with a deep sigh, He said to him, 'Ephphatha!' that is, 'Be opened!'
NASB.35: And his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was removed, and he [began] speaking plainly.
NASB.36: And He gave them orders not to tell anyone; but the more He ordered them, the more widely they continued to proclaim it.
NASB.37: They were utterly astonished, saying, 'He has done all things well; He makes even the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.'

NLT.1: One day some Pharisees and teachers of religious law arrived from Jerusalem to see Jesus.
NLT.2: They noticed that some of his disciples failed to follow the Jewish ritual of hand washing before eating.
NLT.3: (The Jews, especially the Pharisees, do not eat until they have poured water over their cupped hands, as required by their ancient traditions.
NLT.4: Similarly, they don’t eat anything from the market until they immerse their hands in water. This is but one of many traditions they have clung to — such as their ceremonial washing of cups, pitchers, and kettles. )
NLT.5: So the Pharisees and teachers of religious law asked him, “Why don’t your disciples follow our age-old tradition? They eat without first performing the hand-washing ceremony.”
NLT.6:
Jesus replied, “You hypocrites! Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you, for he wrote, ‘These people honor me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me.
NLT.7:
Their worship is a farce,
for they teach man-made ideas as commands from God.’
NLT.8: For you ignore God’s law and substitute your own tradition.”
NLT.9: Then he said, “You skillfully sidestep God’s law in order to hold on to your own tradition.
NLT.10: For instance, Moses gave you this law from God: ‘Honor your father and mother,’ and ‘Anyone who speaks disrespectfully of father or mother must be put to death.’
NLT.11: But you say it is all right for people to say to their parents, ‘Sorry, I can’t help you. For I have vowed to give to God what I would have given to you.’
NLT.12: In this way, you let them disregard their needy parents.
NLT.13: And so you cancel the word of God in order to hand down your own tradition. And this is only one example among many others.”
NLT.14: Then Jesus called to the crowd to come and hear. “All of you listen,” he said, “and try to understand.
NLT.15: It’s not what goes into your body that defiles you; you are defiled by what comes from your heart. ”
NLT.17: Then Jesus went into a house to get away from the crowd, and his disciples asked him what he meant by the parable he had just used.
NLT.18: “Don’t you understand either?” he asked. “Can’t you see that the food you put into your body cannot defile you?
NLT.19: Food doesn’t go into your heart, but only passes through the stomach and then goes into the sewer.” (By saying this, he declared that every kind of food is acceptable in God’s eyes.)
NLT.20: And then he added, “It is what comes from inside that defiles you.
NLT.21: For from within, out of a person’s heart, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder,
NLT.22: adultery, greed, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, envy, slander, pride, and foolishness.
NLT.23: All these vile things come from within; they are what defile you.”
NLT.24: Then Jesus left Galilee and went north to the region of Tyre. He didn’t want anyone to know which house he was staying in, but he couldn’t keep it a secret.
NLT.25: Right away a woman who had heard about him came and fell at his feet. Her little girl was possessed by an evil spirit,
NLT.26: and she begged him to cast out the demon from her daughter. Since she was a Gentile, born in Syrian Phoenicia,
NLT.27: Jesus told her, “First I should feed the children — my own family, the Jews. It isn’t right to take food from the children and throw it to the dogs.”
NLT.28: She replied, “That’s true, Lord, but even the dogs under the table are allowed to eat the scraps from the children’s plates.”
NLT.29: “Good answer!” he said. “Now go home, for the demon has left your daughter.”
NLT.30: And when she arrived home, she found her little girl lying quietly in bed, and the demon was gone.
NLT.31: Jesus left Tyre and went up to Sidon before going back to the Sea of Galilee and the region of the Ten Towns.
NLT.32: A deaf man with a speech impediment was brought to him, and the people begged Jesus to lay his hands on the man to heal him.
NLT.33: Jesus led him away from the crowd so they could be alone. He put his fingers into the man’s ears. Then, spitting on his own fingers, he touched the man’s tongue.
NLT.34: Looking up to heaven, he sighed and said, “Ephphatha,” which means, “Be opened!”
NLT.35: Instantly the man could hear perfectly, and his tongue was freed so he could speak plainly!
NLT.36: Jesus told the crowd not to tell anyone, but the more he told them not to, the more they spread the news.
NLT.37: They were completely amazed and said again and again, “Everything he does is wonderful. He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cannot speak.”

WEB.1: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered together to him, having come from Jerusalem.
WEB.2: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.
WEB.3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews, don’t eat unless they wash their hands and forearms, holding to the tradition of the elders.
WEB.4: They don’t eat when they come from the marketplace unless they bathe themselves, and there are many other things, which they have received to hold to: washings of cups, pitchers, bronze vessels, and couches.)
WEB.5: The Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why don’t your disciples walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with unwashed hands?”
WEB.6: He answered them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written,  ‘This people honors me with their lips,  but their heart is far from me.
WEB.7: But they worship me in vain,  teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’
WEB.8: “For you set aside the commandment of God, and hold tightly to the tradition of men — the washing of pitchers and cups, and you do many other such things.”
WEB.9: He said to them, “Full well do you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.
WEB.10: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother;’  and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’
WEB.11: But you say, ‘If a man tells his father or his mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban,  that is to say, given to God”;’
WEB.12: then you no longer allow him to do anything for his father or his mother,
WEB.13: making void the word of God by your tradition, which you have handed down. You do many things like this.”
WEB.14: He called all the multitude to himself, and said to them, “Hear me, all of you, and understand.
WEB.15: There is nothing from outside of the man, that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man.
WEB.16: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!”
WEB.17: When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable.
WEB.18: He said to them, “Are you also without understanding? Don’t you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can’t defile him,
WEB.19: because it doesn’t go into his heart, but into his stomach, then into the latrine, thus purifying all foods ?”
WEB.20: He said, “That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.
WEB.21: For from within, out of the hearts of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, sexual sins, murders, thefts,
WEB.22: covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness.
WEB.23: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.”
WEB.24: From there he arose, and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house, and didn’t want anyone to know it, but he couldn’t escape notice.
WEB.25: For a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell down at his feet.
WEB.26: Now the woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by race. She begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.
WEB.27: But Jesus said to her, “Let the children be filled first, for it is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”
WEB.28: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
WEB.29: He said to her, “For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter.”
WEB.30: She went away to her house, and found the child having been laid on the bed, with the demon gone out.
WEB.31: Again he departed from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and came to the sea of Galilee, through the middle of the region of Decapolis.
WEB.32: They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.
WEB.33: He took him aside from the multitude, privately, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat, and touched his tongue.
WEB.34: Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him, “Ephphatha!”  that is, “Be opened!”
WEB.35: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was released, and he spoke clearly.
WEB.36: He commanded them that they should tell no one, but the more he commanded them, so much the more widely they proclaimed it.
WEB.37: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear, and the mute speak!”

CSB17.1: The <sup>ⓚ</sup> Pharisees <sup>ⓛ</sup> and some of the scribes <sup>ⓜ</sup> who had come from Jerusalem <sup>ⓝ</sup> gathered around him.
CSB17.2: They observed that some of his disciples were eating bread with unclean <sup>ⓐ</sup>  — that is, unwashed — hands.
CSB17.3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, keeping the tradition of the elders.
CSB17.4: When they come from the marketplace, <sup>ⓑ</sup> they do not eat unless they have washed. <sup>ⓒ</sup> And there are many other customs they have received and keep, like the washing of cups, pitchers, kettles, and dining couches. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>)
CSB17.5: So the Pharisees <sup>ⓔ</sup> and the scribes <sup>ⓕ</sup> asked him, “Why don’t your disciples live according to the tradition of the elders, <sup>ⓖ</sup> instead of eating bread with ceremonially unclean <sup>[2]</sup> hands?”
CSB17.6:
He answered them, “Isaiah ⓗ prophesied ⓘ correctly about you hypocrites, ⓙ as it is written: ⓚ This people honors ⓛ me with their lips,
but their heart is far from me.
CSB17.7:
They worship me in vain,
teaching ⓜ as doctrines ⓝ human commands. [3] ⓐ
CSB17.8: Abandoning the command of God, you hold on to human tradition.” <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.9: He also said to them, “You have a fine way of invalidating God’s command in order to set up <sup>[5]</sup>your tradition!
CSB17.10: For Moses ⓑsaid: Honor your father and your mother; [6] ⓒand Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must be put to death. ⓓ ⓔ
CSB17.11: But you say, ‘If anyone tells his father or mother: Whatever benefit ⓕ you might have received from me is corban’” (that is, an offering ⓖ devoted to God),
CSB17.12: “you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother.
CSB17.13: You nullify the word of God <sup>ⓗ</sup> by your tradition that you have handed <sup>ⓘ</sup> down. And you do many other similar things.”
CSB17.14: Summoning <sup>ⓙ</sup> the crowd again, he told them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand: <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.15: Nothing that goes into a person from outside can defile him but the things that come out of a person are what defile him.” <sup>[7]</sup>
CSB17.17: When he went into the house away from the crowd, his disciples asked him about the parable.
CSB17.18: He said to them, “Are you also as lacking in understanding? Don’t you realize that nothing going into a person from the outside can defile him?
CSB17.19: For it doesn’t go into his heart but into the stomach <sup>ⓛ</sup> and is eliminated” (thus he declared all foods clean <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>).
CSB17.20: And he said, “What comes out of a person is what defiles him.
CSB17.21: For from within, out of people’s hearts, come evil thoughts, sexual immoralities, <sup>ⓝ</sup> thefts, murders, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: All these evil things come from within and defile a person.”
CSB17.24: He got up and departed from <sup>ⓘ</sup> there to the region of Tyre. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> He entered a house and did not want anyone to know it, but he could not escape notice.
CSB17.25: Instead, immediately after hearing about him, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit <sup>ⓚ</sup> came and fell at his feet. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.26: The woman was a Gentile, <sup>[11]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> a Syrophoenician by birth, and she was asking him to cast the demon <sup>ⓝ</sup> out of her daughter.
CSB17.27: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, because it isn’t right to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.28: But she replied to him, “Lord, <sup>ⓑ</sup> even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
CSB17.29: Then he told her, “Because of this reply, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.”
CSB17.30: When she went back to her home, she found her child lying on the bed, and the demon was gone.
CSB17.31: Again, leaving the region of Tyre, <sup>ⓒ</sup> he went by way of Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, <sup>ⓓ</sup> through <sup>[12]</sup> the region of the Decapolis. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.32: They brought to him a deaf <sup>ⓕ</sup> man who had difficulty speaking <sup>ⓖ</sup> and begged Jesus to lay his hand on <sup>ⓗ</sup> him.
CSB17.33: So he took him away from the crowd in private. After putting his fingers in the man’s ears and spitting, <sup>ⓘ</sup> he touched his tongue.
CSB17.34: Looking up to heaven, <sup>ⓙ</sup> he sighed deeply and said to him, “Ephphatha!” <sup>[13]</sup> (that is, “Be opened!”).
CSB17.35: Immediately his ears were opened, <sup>ⓚ</sup> his tongue was loosened, and he began to speak clearly. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.36: He ordered them to tell no one, <sup>ⓜ</sup> but the more he ordered them, the more they proclaimed it. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.37: They were extremely astonished and said, “He has done everything well. <sup>ⓐ</sup> He even makes the deaf <sup>ⓑ</sup> hear and the mute speak.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>

AMP.1: Now the Pharisees and some of the scribes came from Jerusalem and gathered around Him,
AMP.2: and they had seen that some of His disciples ate their bread with [ceremonially] impure hands, that is, unwashed [and defiled according to Jewish religious ritual].
AMP.3: ( For the Pharisees and all of the Jews do not eat unless they carefully wash their hands, holding firmly to the traditions of the elders;
AMP.4: and when they come from the market place, they do not eat unless they cleanse themselves [completely according to ritual]; and there are many other things [oral, man-made laws and traditions handed down to them] which they follow diligently, such as the washing of cups and pitchers and copper utensils.)
AMP.5: So the Pharisees and scribes asked Jesus, "Why do Your disciples not live their lives according to the tradition of the elders, but [instead] eat their bread with [ceremonially] unwashed hands?"
AMP.6:
He replied, "Rightly did Isaiah prophesy about you hypocrites (play-actors, pretenders), as it is written [in Scripture],
'These people honor Me with their lips,
But their heart is far from Me.
AMP.7:
'They worship Me in vain [their worship is meaningless and worthless, a pretense],
Teaching the precepts of men as doctrines [giving their traditions equal weight with the Scriptures].'
AMP.8: You disregard and neglect the commandment of God, and cling [faithfully] to the tradition of men."
AMP.9: He was also saying to them, "You are experts at setting aside and nullifying the commandment of God in order to keep your [man-made] tradition and regulations.
AMP.10: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother [with respect and gratitude]'; and, 'He who speaks evil of his father or mother must be put to death';
AMP.11: but you [Pharisees and scribes] say, 'If a man tells his father or mother, "Whatever I have that would help you is Corban, (that is to say, already a gift to God),"'
AMP.12: then you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother [since helping them would violate his vow of Corban];
AMP.13: so you nullify the [authority of the] word of God [acting as if it did not apply] because of your tradition which you have handed down [through the elders]. And you do many things such as that."
AMP.14: After He called the people to Him again, He began saying to them, "Listen [carefully] to Me, all of you, [hear] and understand [what I am saying]:
AMP.15: there is nothing outside a man [such as food] which by going into him can defile him [morally or spiritually]; but the things which come out of [the heart of] a man are what defile and dishonor him.
AMP.16: ["If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear."]
AMP.17: When Jesus had left the crowd and gone into the house, His disciples asked Him about the parable.
AMP.18: And He said to them, "Are you, too, so foolish and lacking in understanding? Do you not understand that whatever goes into the man from outside cannot defile and dishonor him,
AMP.19: since it does not enter his heart, but [only] his stomach, and [from there it] is eliminated?" (By this, He declared all foods ceremonially clean.)
AMP.20: And He said, "Whatever comes from [the heart of] a man, that is what defiles and dishonors him.
AMP.21: For from within, [that is] out the heart of men, come base and malevolent thoughts and schemes, acts of sexual immorality, thefts, murders, adulteries,
AMP.22: acts of greed and covetousness, wickedness, deceit, unrestrained conduct, envy and jealousy, slander and profanity, arrogance and self-righteousness and foolishness (poor judgment).
AMP.23: All these evil things [schemes and desires] come from within and defile and dishonor the man."
AMP.24: Jesus got up and left there and went to the region of Tyre [and Sidon, the coastal area of Phoenicia]. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know about it; but it was impossible for Him to be hidden [from the public].
AMP.25: Instead, after hearing about Him, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately came and fell at His feet.
AMP.26: Now the woman was a Gentile (Greek), a Syrophoenician by nationality. And she kept pleading with Him to drive the demon out of her daughter.
AMP.27: He was saying to her, "First let the children [of Israel] be fed, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the pet dogs (non-Jews)."
AMP.28: But she replied, "Yes, Lord, but even the pet dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."
AMP.29: And He said to her, "Because of this answer [reflecting your humility and faith], go [knowing that your request is granted]; the demon has left your daughter [permanently]."
AMP.30: And returning to her home, she found the child lying on the couch [relaxed and resting], the demon having gone.
AMP.31: Soon after this Jesus left the region of Tyre, and passed through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, through the region of Decapolis [the ten Hellenistic cities].
AMP.32: They brought to Him a man who was deaf and had difficulty speaking, and they begged Jesus to place His hand on him.
AMP.33: Jesus, taking him aside by himself, away from the crowd, put His fingers into the man's ears, and after spitting, He touched the man's tongue [with the saliva];
AMP.34: and looking up to heaven, He sighed deeply and said to the man, "Ephphatha," which [in Aramaic] means, " Be opened and released!"
AMP.35: And his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he began speaking plainly.
AMP.36: Jesus commanded them not to tell anyone; but the more He ordered them, the more widely they continued to proclaim it.
AMP.37: They were thoroughly astounded and completely overwhelmed, saying, "He has done everything well! He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak!"

MSG.1: The Pharisees, along with some religion scholars who had come from Jerusalem, gathered around him.
MSG.2: They noticed that some of his disciples weren't being careful with ritual washings before meals.
MSG.3: The Pharisees—Jews in general, in fact—would never eat a meal without going through the motions of a ritual hand-washing,
MSG.4: with an especially vigorous scrubbing if they had just come from the market (to say nothing of the scourings they'd give jugs and pots and pans).
MSG.5: The Pharisees and religion scholars asked, "Why do your disciples flout the rules, showing up at meals without washing their hands?"
MSG.6: Jesus answered, "Isaiah was right about frauds like you, hit the bull's-eye in fact: These people make a big show of saying the right thing, but their heart isn't in it.
MSG.7: They act like they are worshiping me, but they don't mean it. They just use me as a cover for teaching whatever suits their fancy,
MSG.8: Ditching God's command and taking up the latest fads."
MSG.9: He went on, "Well, good for you. You get rid of God's command so you won't be inconvenienced in following the religious fashions!
MSG.10: Moses said, 'Respect your father and mother,' and, 'Anyone denouncing father or mother should be killed.'
MSG.11: But you weasel out of that by saying that it's perfectly acceptable to say to father or mother, 'Gift! What I owed you I've given as a gift to God,'
MSG.12: thus relieving yourselves of obligation to father or mother.
MSG.13: You scratch out God's Word and scrawl a whim in its place. You do a lot of things like this."
MSG.14: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, "Listen now, all of you—take this to heart.
MSG.15: It's not what you swallow that pollutes your life; it's what you vomit—that's the real pollution."
MSG.17: When he was back home after being with the crowd, his disciples said, "We don't get it. Put it in plain language."
MSG.18: Jesus said, "Are you being willfully stupid? Don't you see that what you swallow can't contaminate you?
MSG.19: It doesn't enter your heart but your stomach, works its way through the intestines, and is finally flushed." (That took care of dietary quibbling; Jesus was saying that all foods are fit to eat.)
MSG.20: He went on: "It's what comes out of a person that pollutes:
MSG.22: greed, depravity, deceptive dealings, carousing, mean looks, slander, arrogance, foolishness—
MSG.23: all these are vomit from the heart. There is the source of your pollution."
MSG.24: From there Jesus set out for the vicinity of Tyre. He entered a house there where he didn't think he would be found, but he couldn't escape notice.
MSG.25: He was barely inside when a woman who had a disturbed daughter heard where he was. She came and knelt at his feet,
MSG.26: begging for help. The woman was Greek, Syro-Phoenician by birth. She asked him to cure her daughter.
MSG.27: He said, "Stand in line and take your turn. The children get fed first. If there's any left over, the dogs get it."
MSG.28: She said, "Of course, Master. But don't dogs under the table get scraps dropped by the children?"
MSG.29: Jesus was impressed. "You're right! On your way! Your daughter is no longer disturbed. The demonic affliction is gone."
MSG.30: She went home and found her daughter relaxed on the bed, the torment gone for good.
MSG.31: Then he left the region of Tyre, went through Sidon back to Galilee Lake and over to the district of the Ten Towns.
MSG.32: Some people brought a man who could neither hear nor speak and asked Jesus to lay a healing hand on him.
MSG.33: He took the man off by himself, put his fingers in the man's ears and some spit on the man's tongue.
MSG.34: Then Jesus looked up in prayer, groaned mightily, and commanded, "Ephphatha!—Open up!"
MSG.35: And it happened. The man's hearing was clear and his speech plain—just like that.
MSG.36: Jesus urged them to keep it quiet, but they talked it up all the more,
MSG.37: beside themselves with excitement. "He's done it all and done it well. He gives hearing to the deaf, speech to the speechless."

NIV.1:
Clean and Unclean
The Pharisees and some of the teachers of the law who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus and
NIV.2: saw some of his disciples eating food with hands that were “unclean,” that is, unwashed.
NIV.3: (The Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, holding to the tradition of the elders.
NIV.4: When they come from the marketplace they do not eat unless they wash. And they observe many other traditions, such as the washing of cups, pitchers and kettles.)
NIV.5: So the Pharisees and teachers of the law asked Jesus, “Why don’t your disciples live according to the tradition of the elders instead of eating their food with ‘unclean’ hands?”
NIV.6:
He replied, “Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you hypocrites; as it is written:
“‘These people honor me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me.
NIV.7:
They worship me in vain;
their teachings are but rules taught by men.’
NIV.8: You have let go of the commands of God and are holding on to the traditions of men.”
NIV.9: And he said to them: “You have a fine way of setting aside the commands of God in order to observe your own traditions!
NIV.10: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’
NIV.11: But you say that if a man says to his father or mother: ‘Whatever help you might otherwise have received from me is Corban’ (that is, a gift devoted to God),
NIV.12: then you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother.
NIV.13: Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that.”
NIV.14: Again Jesus called the crowd to him and said, “Listen to me, everyone, and understand this.
NIV.15: Nothing outside a man can make him ‘unclean’ by going into him. Rather, it is what comes out of a man that makes him ‘unclean.’”
NIV.17: After he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about this parable.
NIV.18: “Are you so dull?” he asked. “Don’t you see that nothing that enters a man from the outside can make him ‘unclean’?
NIV.19: For it doesn’t go into his heart but into his stomach, and then out of his body.” (In saying this, Jesus declared all foods “clean.”)
NIV.20: He went on: “What comes out of a man is what makes him ‘unclean.’
NIV.21: For from within, out of men’s hearts, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,
NIV.22: greed, malice, deceit, lewdness, envy, slander, arrogance and folly.
NIV.23: All these evils come from inside and make a man ‘unclean.’”
NIV.24:
The Faith of a Syrophoenician Woman
Jesus left that place and went to the vicinity of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know it; yet he could not keep his presence secret.
NIV.25: In fact, as soon as she heard about him, a woman whose little daughter was possessed by an evil spirit came and fell at his feet.
NIV.26: The woman was a Greek, born in Syrian Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to drive the demon out of her daughter.
NIV.27: “First let the children eat all they want,” he told her, “for it is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to their dogs.”
NIV.28: “Yes, Lord,” she replied, “but even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
NIV.29: Then he told her, “For such a reply, you may go; the demon has left your daughter.”
NIV.30: She went home and found her child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
NIV.31:
The Healing of a Deaf and Mute Man
Then Jesus left the vicinity of Tyre and went through Sidon, down to the Sea of Galilee and into the region of the Decapolis.
NIV.32: There some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk, and they begged him to place his hand on the man.
NIV.33: After he took him aside, away from the crowd, Jesus put his fingers into the man’s ears. Then he spit and touched the man’s tongue.
NIV.34: He looked up to heaven and with a deep sigh said to him, “Ephphatha!” (which means, “Be opened!”).
NIV.35: At this, the man’s ears were opened, his tongue was loosened and he began to speak plainly.
NIV.36: Jesus commanded them not to tell anyone. But the more he did so, the more they kept talking about it.
NIV.37: People were overwhelmed with amazement. “He has done everything well,” they said. “He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak.”

NRSVCE.1: Now when the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,
NRSVCE.2: they noticed that some of his disciples were eating with defiled hands, that is, without washing them.
NRSVCE.3: (For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, do not eat unless they thoroughly wash their hands, thus observing the tradition of the elders;
NRSVCE.4: and they do not eat anything from the market unless they wash it; and there are also many other traditions that they observe, the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles. )
NRSVCE.5: So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
NRSVCE.6:
He said to them, “Isaiah prophesied rightly about you hypocrites, as it is written, ‘This people honors me with their lips,
but their hearts are far from me;
NRSVCE.7:
in vain do they worship me,
teaching human precepts as doctrines.’
NRSVCE.8: You abandon the commandment of God and hold to human tradition.”
NRSVCE.9: Then he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition!
NRSVCE.10: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
NRSVCE.11: But you say that if anyone tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is Corban’ (that is, an offering to God ) —
NRSVCE.12: then you no longer permit doing anything for a father or mother,
NRSVCE.13: thus making void the word of God through your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many things like this.”
NRSVCE.14: Then he called the crowd again and said to them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand:
NRSVCE.15: there is nothing outside a person that by going in can defile, but the things that come out are what defile.”
NRSVCE.17: When he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
NRSVCE.18: He said to them, “Then do you also fail to understand? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile,
NRSVCE.19: since it enters, not the heart but the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
NRSVCE.20: And he said, “It is what comes out of a person that defiles.
NRSVCE.21: For it is from within, from the human heart, that evil intentions come: fornication, theft, murder,
NRSVCE.22: adultery, avarice, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, envy, slander, pride, folly.
NRSVCE.23: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
NRSVCE.24: From there he set out and went away to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. Yet he could not escape notice,
NRSVCE.25: but a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him, and she came and bowed down at his feet.
NRSVCE.26: Now the woman was a Gentile, of Syrophoenician origin. She begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
NRSVCE.27: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
NRSVCE.28: But she answered him, “Sir, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
NRSVCE.29: Then he said to her, “For saying that, you may go — the demon has left your daughter.”
NRSVCE.30: So she went home, found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
NRSVCE.31: Then he returned from the region of Tyre, and went by way of Sidon towards the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
NRSVCE.32: They brought to him a deaf man who had an impediment in his speech; and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
NRSVCE.33: He took him aside in private, away from the crowd, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat and touched his tongue.
NRSVCE.34: Then looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
NRSVCE.35: And immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
NRSVCE.36: Then Jesus ordered them to tell no one; but the more he ordered them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
NRSVCE.37: They were astounded beyond measure, saying, “He has done everything well; he even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”

NET.1: Now the Pharisees and some of the experts in the law who came from Jerusalem gathered around him.
NET.2: And they saw that some of Jesus’ disciples ate their bread with unclean hands, that is, unwashed.
NET.3: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they perform a ritual washing, holding fast to the tradition of the elders.
NET.4: And when they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash. They hold fast to many other traditions: the washing of cups, pots, kettles, and dining couches. )
NET.5: The Pharisees and the experts in the law asked him, “Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with unwashed hands?”
NET.6: He said to them, “Isaiah prophesied correctly about you hypocrites, as it is written: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
NET.7: They worship me in vain, teaching as doctrine the commandments of men.’
NET.8: Having no regard for the command of God, you hold fast to human tradition.”
NET.9: He also said to them, “You neatly reject the commandment of God in order to set up your tradition.
NET.10: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Whoever insults his father or mother must be put to death.’
NET.11: But you say that if anyone tells his father or mother, ‘Whatever help you would have received from me is corban’ (that is, a gift for God),
NET.12: then you no longer permit him to do anything for his father or mother.
NET.13: Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like this.”
NET.14: Then he called the crowd again and said to them, “Listen to me, everyone, and understand.
NET.15: There is nothing outside of a person that can defile him by going into him. Rather, it is what comes out of a person that defiles him.”
NET.17: Now when Jesus had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
NET.18: He said to them, “Are you so foolish? Don’t you understand that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him?
NET.19: For it does not enter his heart but his stomach, and then goes out into the sewer.” (This means all foods are clean.)
NET.20: He said, “What comes out of a person defiles him.
NET.21: For from within, out of the human heart, come evil ideas, sexual immorality, theft, murder,
NET.22: adultery, greed, evil, deceit, debauchery, envy, slander, pride, and folly.
NET.23: All these evils come from within and defile a person.”
NET.24: After Jesus left there, he went to the region of Tyre. When he went into a house, he did not want anyone to know, but he was not able to escape notice.
NET.25: Instead, a woman whose young daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him and came and fell at his feet.
NET.26: The woman was a Greek, of Syrophoenician origin. She asked him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
NET.27: He said to her, “Let the children be satisfied first, for it is not right to take the children’s bread and to throw it to the dogs.”
NET.28: She answered, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
NET.29: Then he said to her, “Because you said this, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.”
NET.30: She went home and found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
NET.31: Then Jesus went out again from the region of Tyre and came through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee in the region of the Decapolis.
NET.32: They brought to him a deaf man who had difficulty speaking, and they asked him to place his hands on him.
NET.33: After Jesus took him aside privately, away from the crowd, he put his fingers in the man’s ears, and after spitting, he touched his tongue.
NET.34: Then he looked up to heaven and said with a sigh, “Ephphatha” (that is, “Be opened”).
NET.35: And immediately the man’s ears were opened, his tongue loosened, and he spoke plainly.
NET.36: Jesus ordered them not to tell anything. But as much as he ordered them not to do this, they proclaimed it all the more.
NET.37: People were completely astounded and said, “He has done everything well. He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak.”
